danger_signals.visual_form: Kilmaat creature designs and alien aesthetic elements within Egyptian setting
danger_signals.meaning: Non-human threat; world has been invaded/corrupted by foreign force

danger_signals.visual_form: Reanimated corpses, wrapped figures, spectral entities
danger_signals.meaning: Desecration of sacred tombs; supernatural corruption of ancient site
Guardian creatures weaponized; sacred imagery perverted to threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Collapsed structures, breached tombs, corrupted temple zones
danger_signals.meaning: Ongoing damage and chaos in previously stable world; escalating crisis
Natural world corrupted; spiritual realm breached into physical domain

safety_signals.visual_form: Spectral manifestation of pharaoh providing guidance and direction
safety_signals.meaning: Allied supernatural force; legitimate authority supporting player mission

safety_signals.visual_form: Intact temples, tombs, hieroglyphic markers, statues
safety_signals.meaning: Protected zones; spaces of order and cultural continuity

safety_signals.visual_form: Standard FPS resource items scattered in environment
safety_signals.meaning: Player survival support; indication of accessible safe resources

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various creature types including standard warriors, specialized minions, boss entities (Set god form)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Corrupted temple zones, alien technology integrated into Egyptian structures, mothership final level

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pharaonic imagery, Ramses mummy, Anubis iconography, hieroglyphics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Ramses presents as both corrupted threat (when Kilmaat-controlled) and ally (when spirit-guided)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Valley of Karnak, tombs, temples, sacred chambers, pyramid structures

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crack team military aesthetic, modern weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Downed helicopter introduction, radio transmission device collection mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Intact Egyptian monuments, sealed tombs, undisturbed sacred spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Static environment; no active threats in these zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien creatures throughout valley, corrupted temple zones, exhumed tombs, technological elements mixed with ancient structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Constant hostile enemy presence; escalating difficulty; creatures actively patrolling and defending territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pharaoh mummy as corrupted boss entity; alien influence over sacred artifacts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mid-game boss encounter (Set god form); player must confront corrupted authority figure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kilmaat mothership with armed nuclear weapon; time-limited environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: 15-minute countdown timer; escalating urgency; final stage on alien vessel

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player victory outcome A - Good ending (console versions)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Rescued from collapsing tomb; ascension to pharaonic status; divine blessing visual reward
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escape sequence; transformation into world ruler; narrative resolution

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Player defeat outcome - Bad ending (console versions)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Burial in Ramses' tomb; entombment as human artifact in Kilmaat-ruled future
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player trapped; historical reversal; humanity extinct or subjugated

environmental_storytelling.example: Exhumed tombs and breached temples communicate that invaders have violated Egyptian cultural/spiritual sanctity
Intact temple structures signal protected/stable zones; collapsed or corrupted structures indicate danger zones
Radio transmission device pieces scattered through world; player must collect eight to determine good ending, creating spatial narrative of escape preparation
Kilmaat technology integrated into Egyptian monuments visualizes cultural collision and invasion narrative
Set god and controlled Ramses represent perversion of legitimate power, requiring player to restore or destroy

Metroidvania-style progression (lava-walking, levitation, underwater breathing, force field passage) uses environment as storytelling device showing player integration with world's supernatural/physical forces

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active hostility toward player; territory defense
Undead servant behavior; predictable attack patterns; slow movement
Provides directional guidance; offers narrative context; supports player mission
High-threat combat encounter; represents peak Kilmaat control; defeat signals player progress toward world restoration
Combat-focused; represent perverted sacred power; stronger than standard minions

notes: PowerSlave uses Egyptian archaeological setting as core thematic framework. Danger signals heavily leverage sacred desecration (tombs exhumed, gods corrupted, mummies reanimated) as primary threat communication. World state manifests through both visual environment (intact vs. corrupted temples) and narrative/ending states (two-path conclusion in console versions reflects player agency in world outcome). Faction conflict structured as invasion narrative: legitimate Egyptian authority (Ramses, sacred spaces) vs. alien occupiers (Kilmaat), with player as external human agent affecting outcome. Metroidvania progression mechanics double as environmental storytelling, with supernatural abilities marking player's gradual integration into world's spiritual/physical systems. Nuclear destruction outcome (MS-DOS path) adds existential stakes to invasion narrative.
